TaskDescription: Daytime nap with transcranial temporal interference (TI) stimulation delivered in counterbalanced 40-second blocks (5 trials per block, 8 s per trial) of three conditions. Active conditions: 1 Hz envelope (2000 + 2001 Hz carriers) and 5 Hz envelope (2000 + 2005 Hz carriers), 2 mA zero-to-peak per channel pair. Sham (OFF) condition: both channel pairs at 0 Hz and 0 mA - stimulation was fully ramped down, no carrier was active. Stimulation electrodes: frontal pair F7-F8, parietal pair P5-P6. Transitions between conditions involving a parameter change were preceded by a 5-second linear ramp-down and followed by a 5-second linear ramp-up at the prevailing carrier frequency. Participants were instructed to attempt to sleep as during a typical daytime nap.
Instructions: Participants were asked to attempt to fall asleep. Stimulation was titrated below the perceptual threshold and confirmed imperceptible at the experimental 2 mA intensity prior to the nap.
SubjectArtefactDescription: Carrier-frequency ramps (5 s) and small switching transients at condition transitions are marked in events with stim_phase = rampingUp / rampingDown / refract. The hardware low-pass anti-aliasing filter attenuates the 2000 Hz TI carrier prior to digitisation.
ElectricalStimulationParameters: Transcranial temporal interference (TI) stimulation via two electrode pairs (frontal F7-F8, parietal P5-P6; 1.5 cm round rubber electrodes applied with Ten20 conductive paste; TI Solutions AG TIBS-R V3.0). Conditions - OFF: sham, both channel pairs fully ramped down (0 Hz carriers, 0 mA, no current); 1HZ: 2000+2001 Hz carriers, 1 Hz envelope, 2 mA zero-to-peak per pair; 5HZ: 2000+2005 Hz carriers, 5 Hz envelope, 2 mA zero-to-peak per pair. Current measure: zero-to-peak. Trial structure: 8 s trials, 5 trials per block, 40 s blocks. Parameter-change transitions were bracketed by 5 s linear ramps at the prevailing carrier frequency (ramp-down before, ramp-up after); transitions between same-condition blocks were not ramped. Per-condition parameters are also recorded per trial in events.tsv (trial_type, stim_phase) and described in TaskDescription and the dataset README.
